_note: ILLUSTRATIVE Bid-Evaluator scoring params — hand-seeded, NOT calibrated. Same status as dsm_params.json / refineries.json (see CLAUDE.md). The evaluator ranks bids on lowest TOTAL ECONOMIC IMPACT, not lowest sticker price: total_cost_per_bbl = price_per_bbl + grade_mismatch_penalty (if no affected refinery can run the grade) + a delivery-risk uplift (price x f/(1-f) where f is the delivery corridor's twin disruption fraction — the expected cost per DELIVERED barrel; disrupted_route_penalty_usd is only the flat fallback when the fraction is unknown) + transit_days x impact_per_day. impact_per_day = delay_cost.base_per_bbl_per_day + urgency x delay_cost.urgency_extra_per_bbl_per_day, where urgency in [0,1] scales with SCTD's shortfall severity (twin critical/stressed refinery counts) PLUS SPR pressure: spr_weight x max(0, 1 - spr_days_of_cover/spr_comfort_days). stressed_refinery_weight is deliberately small so a broad-but-mild stress field cannot saturate urgency to catastrophe levels (12 stressed = 0.72, not 1.0) — only critical refineries can max it out. Calibrate before treating as real.
